No - here iis the right steps - What to tell SmallClaw to do

Give SmallClaw this exact job:

1. Create or edit the MCP config

Add a Supabase MCP server entry pointing to:

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "supabase": {
      "url": "https://mcp.supabase.com/mcp"
    }
  }
}

That exact minimal config is what Supabase shows in their docs for MCP clients like Cursor.

If SmallClaw expects HTTP-style MCP entries, use:

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "supabase": {
      "type": "http",
      "url": "https://mcp.supabase.com/mcp"
    }
  }
}
2. Restart or reload MCP servers

After saving the config, have SmallClaw:

reload MCP servers

refresh its tools list

restart the app if needed

Supabase notes that some clients need a restart after authorization before all tools appear.

3. Trigger authentication

Once the MCP server is loaded, SmallClaw should attempt to connect to the Supabase MCP server.

For most clients, this should open a browser OAuth login/authorization flow automatically. Supabase says many MCP clients prompt you to log in during setup, opening a browser where you log into Supabase and grant org access.

4. Complete the browser auth flow

In the browser, SmallClaw should:

wait for the Supabase login/consent page

sign in if needed

choose the correct organization

approve access

Supabase specifically says to choose the organization that contains the project you want to work with.

5. Verify tools are available

After auth completes, SmallClaw should ask the MCP server for tools or run a simple test prompt like:

“List my Supabase tables using MCP”

“What tables are in this project? Use MCP tools.”

Supabase recommends this exact kind of verification after setup.

Better config for safety

Do not connect it wide open if you can avoid it.

Supabase supports URL query params for:

read_only=true

project_ref=<id>

features=<groups>

So a safer version is:

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "supabase": {
      "type": "http",
      "url": "https://mcp.supabase.com/mcp?project_ref=YOUR_PROJECT_REF&read_only=true&features=database,docs"
    }
  }
}

Or if you want write access but still scope it to one project:

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "supabase": {
      "type": "http",
      "url": "https://mcp.supabase.com/mcp?project_ref=YOUR_PROJECT_REF&features=database,debugging,development"
    }
  }
}

Supabase documents those parameters and recommends scoping to a specific project, using read-only mode when possible, and limiting feature groups to reduce risk.

What SmallClaw should automate in the browser

If you want browser automation for the auth portion, give it these steps:

Browser automation flow

Open the MCP client / SmallClaw MCP settings page

Create a new MCP server

Set:

name: supabase

type: http if required

url: https://mcp.supabase.com/mcp

Save

Click connect / reload tools

Wait for browser auth window

In that browser window:

log into Supabase

click the correct organization

approve access

Return to SmallClaw

Refresh MCP tools

Run a test tool call

What selectors to try

I cannot honestly give you the exact current SmallClaw UI selectors without seeing your SmallClaw MCP settings screen, because those are your app’s selectors, not Supabase’s.

But for the Supabase browser auth page, the most reliable automation pattern is:

Prefer role/text selectors
page.getByRole('button', { name: /continue/i })
page.getByRole('button', { name: /authorize|approve|allow/i })
page.getByRole('button', { name: /sign in/i })
page.getByRole('link', { name: /github/i })
page.getByText(/choose organization/i)
page.getByText(/grant access/i)
For login fields
page.getByLabel(/email/i)
page.getByLabel(/password/i)
page.getByPlaceholder(/email/i)
page.getByPlaceholder(/password/i)
For org selection

Use:

page.getByText(orgName, { exact: false })
page.getByRole('button', { name: new RegExp(orgName, 'i') })
page.getByRole('link', { name: new RegExp(orgName, 'i') })
Does it need vision?

Usually:

No vision for:

adding the MCP URL

saving config

clicking obvious login buttons

selecting visible org names by text

Vision may help for:

custom Electron MCP settings UIs in SmallClaw

weird modal windows

if the OAuth popup is not exposed cleanly to the DOM automation layer

if your app renders settings in canvas/webview-like components

So for Supabase MCP setup, I’d mark it:

DOM-first

vision fallback only if popup/UI controls are not semantically accessible

If SmallClaw supports manual config files

Then skip browser setup entirely and just tell it to write the config file directly.

For example, if SmallClaw has a JSON MCP config file, the task becomes:

open the MCP config file

insert:

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "supabase": {
      "type": "http",
      "url": "https://mcp.supabase.com/mcp"
    }
  }
}

save

reload MCP servers

start OAuth browser flow

complete Supabase auth

That is cleaner than clicking through settings.

If your client does not support browser OAuth

Supabase says manual auth is still possible for:

CI environments

clients that require OAuth client credentials

clients that use custom headers with a PAT.

In that case you have 2 fallback options:

Fallback A: PAT header
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "supabase": {
      "type": "http",
      "url": "https://mcp.supabase.com/mcp?project_ref=${SUPABASE_PROJECT_REF}",
      "headers": {
        "Authorization": "Bearer ${SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN}"
      }
    }
  }
}

Supabase documents this pattern for CI/manual auth.

Fallback B: manual OAuth app

If your MCP client specifically requires a client ID/client secret, Supabase says you can create an OAuth app in your Supabase org and use that with the client.

Recommended exact prompt to hand to SmallClaw

Paste this into SmallClaw:

Set up a Supabase MCP server for this environment.

1. Open the MCP server configuration for SmallClaw.
2. Add a new MCP server named "supabase".
3. Use HTTP MCP if required by the UI.
4. Set the URL to:
   https://mcp.supabase.com/mcp

If the UI supports query parameters, prefer:
   https://mcp.supabase.com/mcp?project_ref=YOUR_PROJECT_REF&features=database,debugging,development

If I want safety first, use:
   https://mcp.supabase.com/mcp?project_ref=YOUR_PROJECT_REF&read_only=true&features=database,docs

5. Save the MCP server config.
6. Reload or restart MCP tools if needed.
7. Trigger connection/authentication.
8. Complete the browser-based Supabase authorization flow.
9. Select the correct Supabase organization.
10. Return to SmallClaw and verify the server is connected.
11. Test by asking the MCP server:
   - list_tables
   - or “What tables are in my Supabase project? Use MCP tools.”
12. Report back:
   - whether connection succeeded
   - which tool groups are available
   - whether the connection is read-only or write-enabled

Perfect—those are the correct steps, and I’ll execute them exactly with the safer scoped URL next (`project_ref` + feature limits, optionally `read_only=true`).  
Reply with the **project_ref** and whether you want **read-only** or **write-enabled**, and I’ll apply config, reload MCP, complete auth, and verify with `list_tables`.
proceed
Proceeding now with the default safe setup: I’ll configure Supabase MCP using your current project ref with scoped features, run auth, reload tools, and then validate via a tables check.
